metadata_information.title: Does paternity leave matter for female employment in developing economies evidence from firm data
metadata_information.version: Python script, from D&R API, generated on 2026-03-19

title_statement.title: Does paternity leave matter for female employment in developing economies evidence from firm data

document_description.abstract: For a sample of 53 developing countries, the results show that women's employment among private firms is significantly higher in countries that mandate paternity leave versus those that do not. A conservative estimate suggests an increase of 6.8 percentage points in the proportion of women workers associated with the mandating of paternity leave.
